One of the best Prosecco Superiore as always.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Superiore di Cartizze DOCG La Rivetta
TESTO_COMMENTO: Easily a contender of blanc des blancs from any French producer. Minuscule hill in Veneto, it was a lovely visit to their cellar built out of WWI tunnels.  White flowers (jasmine), crushed stones, a faint lychee on the nose. No oaking thank God. White peaches, elegant minerality, banana, melon, young apricots, very light citrusy acidity, subtle bitterness, definitely dry but with a sweet note. Yes a bit of brioche.  Would choose this dude any day against non-pinot noir-dominant champagne

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Superiore di Cartizze DOCG La Rivetta

But I didn�t know that when I tasted it: it�s rich on the nose, fruity and sweet, with some lemon jumping out of the acidity. Very good, Cartizze (now a synonym for Dry but it�s also the name of a hill that is in fact a 1.000-foot-high vineyard of 107 hectares of vines, owned by 140 growers, yes I did my homework).

TESTO_COMMENTO: [WSET D4 Sparkling Blind tasting class(3)] A delicate and refresh Glera 100% Cartizze Sparkling. Cartizze is the most prestigious vineyard area within the Conegliano Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG zone in Veneto, Italy. Pale lemon color. M+ intense nose.  Notes of lemon, ripe apple, pear, white peach, melon, hawthorn, thyme, almond.  Off-dry, M+ acidity, M alcohol(11.5%), M+ body, M+ flavor intensity, M+ finish, M+ complexity. Not suitable for bottle ageing.
